questions.text: The necessary condition for a motion to be Simple Harmonic (SHM) is:
questions.explanation: Restoring force must be proportional to displacement and opposite in direction.

questions.text: The time period of a simple pendulum depends on:
questions.explanation: T = 2π sqrt(L/g); it is independent of mass and amplitude (for small angles).

questions.text: The velocity of a particle in SHM is maximum at:
questions.explanation: v = ω sqrt(A² - x²). At x=0, v is max (ωA).

questions.text: The total energy of a particle performing SHM is:
questions.explanation: E = ½ m ω² A².

questions.text: The phase difference between velocity and acceleration in SHM is:
questions.explanation: Velocity leads displacement by π/2, and acceleration leads velocity by π/2.

questions.text: A second's pendulum has a time period of:
questions.explanation: It takes 1 second for one swing, so a full oscillation (back and forth) takes 2 seconds.

questions.text: If the length of a simple pendulum is increased by 4 times, its time period:
questions.explanation: T ∝ sqrt(L). sqrt(4) = 2.

questions.text: The motion of a simple pendulum is SHM only for:
questions.explanation: Requirement for sin θ ≈ θ approximation.

questions.text: At extreme positions in SHM, which quantity is zero?
questions.explanation: The particle stops momentarily at extreme points before reversing.

questions.text: The frequency of a spring-mass system is f. If mass is quadrupled, frequency becomes:
questions.explanation: f = (1/2π) sqrt(k/m). f ∝ 1/sqrt(m).

questions.text: Restoring force in SHM is maximum at:
questions.explanation: F = -kx. Max x means max F.

questions.text: Acceleration of a particle in SHM is proportional to:
questions.explanation: a = -ω²x.

questions.text: The potential energy in SHM is maximum at:
questions.explanation: U = ½ kx². Max at max x.

questions.text: Kinetic energy in SHM is maximum at:
questions.explanation: Speed is max at mean position.

questions.text: Phase difference between displacement and acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration is in opposite direction to displacement.

questions.text: Differential equation of SHM is:
questions.explanation: Standard linear ODE.

questions.text: Time period of a simple pendulum at the center of Earth is:
questions.explanation: g = 0 at center, so T = 2π sqrt(L/0) = ∞.

questions.text: If mass is attached to two parallel springs k1, k2, effective k is:
questions.explanation: Springs in parallel add up.

questions.text: A girl is swinging in a sitting position. If she stands up, the period:
questions.explanation: Standing up raises CM, decreasing effective length L. T decreases.

questions.text: Example of a non-periodic motion:
questions.explanation: Projectile follows a path once and doesn't repeat.

questions.text: The frequency of oscillation of a simple pendulum in a freely falling lift is:
questions.explanation: Effective g = 0, so T = ∞, f = 1/T = 0.

questions.text: At mean position, total energy is equal to:
questions.explanation: PE is zero at mean position (assuming standard reference).

questions.text: Damped oscillations result in decrease of _____ with time.
questions.explanation: Energy is lost due to friction/drag.

questions.text: Forced oscillations occur when:
questions.explanation: System oscillates at the frequency of driver.

questions.text: Resonance occurs when driver frequency is _____ natural frequency.
questions.explanation: Results in maximum amplitude.

questions.text: Relationship between ω, T and f is:
questions.explanation: Angular frequency definitions.

questions.text: In SHM, acceleration is zero at:
questions.explanation: a = -ω²x. At x=0, a=0.

questions.text: SI unit of force constant k is:
questions.explanation: F = kx => k = F/x.

questions.text: Energy in SHM is dissipated due to:
questions.explanation: Damping forces like air resistance.

questions.text: Graph between PE and displacement in SHM is a:
questions.explanation: U = ½ kx² is quadratic.

questions.text: Graph between Total Energy and displacement is a:
questions.explanation: TE is constant.

questions.text: If two SHMs of same frequency are added, the resultant is:
questions.explanation: Superposition of same-frequency SHMs is SHM.

questions.text: Length of seconds pendulum on Earth is approx:
Oscillatory motion is always periodic, but periodic motion may not be oscillatory.
questions.explanation: Uniform circular motion is periodic but not oscillatory.

questions.text: Effective g in an elevator accelerating up with 'a' is:
questions.explanation: Pseudo force adds to weight.

questions.text: Time period of a spring-mass system on Moon:
questions.explanation: T = 2π sqrt(m/k) does not depend on g.

questions.text: Phase of an oscillation is measured in:
questions.explanation: It describes the state of motion at a given time.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of resonance?
options: Shattering of glass by a singer
questions.explanation: Classic resonance phenomena.

questions.text: Restoring force is also called:
questions.explanation: In springs, it is the elastic force.

questions.text: Amplitude of a forced oscillator at resonance is limited by:
questions.explanation: Without damping, amplitude would be infinite.
